title: taraxacum majoricense
intro: Taraxacum majoricense, also known as the Major dandelion, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of North America. It is a member of the sunflower family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which contain inulin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, liver and kidney disorders, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties. Herbal preparations of Taraxacum majoricense include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to promote digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
